idea.hook: How flat is OUR ENTIRE GALAXY?
facts: The Milky Way galaxy is a vast disk, roughly 100,000 light-years across.
But its main stellar disk is incredibly thin, only about 1,000 light-years thick.
This means its diameter is 100 times greater than its thickness!
idea.payoff: That's flatter than a CD or a perfectly cooked pancake! Our galaxy is an unbelievably thin cosmic sheet.
idea.title: Our Galaxy's SHOCKING Flatness! 🥞🌌

scenes.text: How flat is OUR ENTIRE GALAXY?
scenes.visual: A wide shot of a spiral galaxy (Milky Way-like) against a starfield. Gradually zooms in slightly as text appears.
An illustrative overhead view of a galaxy disk. A measurement line appears horizontally across its diameter, with the text superimposed.
The galaxy illustration rotates to show a side profile, emphasizing its thinness. A small vertical measurement line highlights the thickness.
A simple infographic with a very long, thin rectangle representing the diameter, and a tiny, almost invisible line representing the thickness. Numbers '100' and '1' appear next to them.

scenes.text: Flatter than a CD or a pancake! An unbelievably thin cosmic sheet.
scenes.visual: Quick transitions: an outline of a CD, then a pancake, then back to a highly stylized, extremely thin side-view of the Milky Way, almost a single glowing line.
